questions.q: A feasible region of a linear programming problem is a polygon.?
questions.explanation: The feasible region of a linear programming problem is formed by the intersection of constraints and is always a polygon.

questions.q: A linear programming problem involves?
questions.explanation: A linear programming problem can be either maximization or minimization of a linear function.

questions.q: The corner point of the feasible region is obtained by solving the equations formed by the constraints at the corners.?
questions.explanation: Corner points are obtained by solving the equations formed by the constraints at the corners.

questions.q: A linear function is a function of the form ax + by + c, where a, b and c are constants.?
opts: (D) It depends on a, b and c

questions.q: The objective function is a function to be maximized or minimized.?
questions.explanation: The objective function is a function to be maximized or minimized.

questions.q: A linear programming problem has a unique optimal solution.?
questions.explanation: A linear programming problem may have multiple or no optimal solutions.
